ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as one of the few modern horror films that genuinely terrifies viewers and leaves a lasting sense of dread.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Highly discussed as a masterpiece of revenge cinema and a pivotal film in Park Chan-Wook's career.

ranked_movies.title: 12 Angry Men
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively analyzed as the quintessential legal drama and a masterclass in claustrophobic storytelling and character dynamics.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently praised as a top-tier horror masterpiece, with discussions focusing on its practical effects and enduring legacy.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Deeply analyzed for its themes, direction, and place in true-crime cinema, especially in light of the real killer being caught.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a foundational horror film that, while not scary for some, is universally respected and discussed.

ranked_movies.title: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World
ranked_movies.reason: Held up as the gold standard for historical accuracy, immersion, and non-bullshit filmmaking in the genre.

ranked_movies.title: Talk to Me
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a recent horror film that successfully scared many viewers and is praised for its themes of grief and peer pressure.

ranked_movies.title: Heat
ranked_movies.reason: Cited for its iconic diner scene where the protagonist and antagonist show mutual respect, a key topic of discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Climax
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a prime example of a film where the situation escalates abruptly and chaotically from 1 to 100.

ranked_movies.title: Lawrence of Arabia
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among the favorites for epic, serious historical filmmaking before WWII.

ranked_movies.title: The Town
ranked_movies.reason: Surprise hit discussed for its quality, especially as an example of Ben Affleck's strong directing and writing.

ranked_movies.title: Green Room
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a tense thriller where things go very wrong very quickly.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Named as a top choice for a film that is brilliantly made but emotionally devastating and life-ruining.

ranked_movies.title: Barry Lyndon
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a masterfully crafted and historically accurate film similar to Master and Commander.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as an extremely dark and shocking film that fits the request for movies that require therapy to recover from.

ranked_movies.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a life-affirming film that can change one's perspective and spark excitement for life.

ranked_movies.title: Dredd
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that surprised viewers by being far better than expected.

ranked_movies.title: The Fourth Kind
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a horror film that can be effective by tapping into a viewer's specific, real-life fears.

ranked_movies.title: The Blair Witch Project
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its historical impact and ability to genuinely terrify audiences, especially upon its original release.
